message:
perf(vdbe): re-add ensure_storage_cursor_row_layout early-return after callsite fix

Re-applies the early-return added in 34a61a0b and reverted in
9dd7bc53. The intervening fix on the caller side ensures the
preconditions for the fast path (cursor not freshly refreshed, row
decode populated, payload buffer covers requested span) actually
imply "no work to do" — the eager-values-state staleness that
prompted the revert is now reset by the caller before
ensure_storage_cursor_row_layout is reached, so returning
`eager_values_ready: false` here no longer surfaces stale state to
the consumer.

Behavior in the no-op case:

```rust
if !refreshed
    && !cursor.row_decode.is_empty()
    && cursor.payload_buf.len() >= min_payload_bytes
{
    return Ok(DecodeCacheRefreshState {
        refreshed: false,
        eager_values_ready: false,
    });
}
```

`refreshed = false` and `eager_values_ready = false` mirror what the
slow path would have returned without doing any actual work, so
hot-loop callers that read multiple rows under a single cursor pass
skip the buffer-resize / payload-refetch bookkeeping in the steady
state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
